activities.markdown:
# Note Values in Drumming

Note values define how long (or how fast) notes are:

| Note | Duration | Count |
|------|----------|-------|
| Whole note | 4 beats | 1 — — — |
| Half note | 2 beats | 1 — |
| Quarter note | 1 beat | 1, 2, 3, 4 |
| 8th note | ½ beat | 1+2+3+4+ |
| 16th note | ¼ beat | 1e+a2e+a |

Most grooves use 8th and 16th notes. Quarter notes appear in slow songs.

activities.question: How many 16th notes fit in one beat?
activities.explanation: One beat = 2 eighth notes = 4 sixteenth notes. 16th notes are counted: 1-e-and-a (four subdivisions per beat).
